text:
## Headline

Nimbus management used its earnings call to reiterate fiscal 2026 revenue guidance, raise the full-year non-GAAP operating margin outlook, and detail integration plans for the pending Cirrus Analytics acquisition.

## Q&A signals

- On Cirrus integration, CEO Daniel Cho said native query capabilities would be "generally available inside the Stratus console by the end of fiscal Q4 2026" [Q1], with early modelling pointing to "roughly 40 million dollars of incremental ARR" in fiscal 2027 [Q1].
- On the margin raise, CFO Helena Vargas attributed "about 60 basis points to the hiring slowdown" and "about 40 basis points to underlying gross-margin improvement in Stratus" [Q2].
- On competitive positioning, Cho said win rate against hyperscaler-native offerings "remained above 75 percent in Q2 in competitive deals we tracked" [Q7], adding that Nimbus has "not lost a single seven-figure customer to a hyperscaler-native tool in the last twelve months" [Q7].

## Commitments

- Management reiterated full-year fiscal 2026 revenue guidance of $2.55–$2.60 billion [K2] and raised the non-GAAP operating margin outlook to approximately 19% from a prior 18% [K3].
- Management guided to positive GAAP operating income for full-year fiscal 2026, which would be a first for the company [K4].
- Management expects to close the Cirrus Analytics acquisition by the end of fiscal Q3 2026 [K1], with native Cirrus query capabilities in the Stratus console by end of fiscal Q4 2026 [K5] and roughly $40 million of incremental ARR cross-sell in fiscal 2027 [K6].
